title: How South Korea came to dominate League of Legends
summary: How South Korea came to dominate League of Legends  Red Bull
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'How South Korea came to dominate League of Legends'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

Riot Games continues to refine the competitive structure, balancing the game's high skill ceiling with spectator entertainment. The professional scene is an ever-evolving battleground where even the smallest adjustment can make or break a team's championship aspirations. Every coach, analyst, and player must stay ahead of the curve to remain competitive.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'How South Korea came to dominate League of Legends  Red Bull' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.

title: T1 continue strong start at Mid-Season Invitational
summary: T1 continue strong start at Mid-Season Invitational  Field Level Media
content:
T1 completed a 3-0 sweep of Karmine Corp on Monday to advance to Round 4 of the play-in stage at the League of Legends Mid-Season Invitational in Daejeon, South Korea.

T1 completed the Round 2 wins in 31 minutes, 35 minutes and 27 minutes. Karmine Corp dropped to Round 3 to face Team Liquid, with the winner meeting T1.

The play-in stage runs through Wednesday, with four teams competing in a double-elimination bracket. Teams are seeded according to results from the First Stand Tournament and are playing best-of-five matches. The eventual winner goes to the bracket stage, an eight-team tournament that provides a $2 million prize pool including a $500,000 check for the first-place finisher.

Soo-hwan “Peyz” Kim led T1 with 30 kills against Karmine Corp, which got 13 kills each from Chang-dong “Canna” Kim and Martin “Yike” Sundelin.

Team Liquid swept into Round 3 by eliminating Deep Cross Gaming, winning in 34, 33 and 25 minutes on Monday.

Brandon Joel “Josedeodo” Villegas and Hyeon-seung “Quid” Lim paced Team Liquid with 19 kills each. Three players had 10 kills in a losing effort for DCG.

title: Expanding our Global Esports Distribution with KICK
summary: Expanding our Global Esports Distribution with KICK  Riot Games
content:
We’re welcoming KICK to our global roster of streaming platforms, giving players around the world more ways to watch, co-stream, and engage with our games and esports. KICK is the primary platform for many passionate esports fans, especially in LATAM, MENA, and Europe. Bringing KICK into the mix of our global platform lineup helps us better serve those communities and brings more players together around the moments, matches, and creators they care about most.

Starting with MSI 2026, KICK will carry live broadcasts for global and regional events across LoL Esports, VALORANT, and TFT (excluding China and Korea).

In addition to expanding where fans can watch, this partnership also opens up more opportunities for creators. KICK streamers will have new ways to grow their communities and connect with Riot through ourexpanding creator and partner programsacross League, VALORANT, TFT, and Wild Rift.

The viewing experience on KICK will feel familiar to players, with access to the same drops and incentives available during our global event broadcasts on other platforms. As for KICK  streamers, we’ll also apply the same moderation practices and standards creators on other platforms are held to, ensuring a safe and positive experience for all.

title: Korea e-Sports Association Announces '2026 LoL KeSPA CUP' to Kick Off July 20, Exclusively on Disney+
summary: Korea e-Sports Association Announces '2026 LoL KeSPA CUP' to Kick Off July 20, Exclusively on Disney+  invenglobal.com
content:
The Korea e-Sports Association (KeSPA) officially announced on the 30th that the 2026 LoL KeSPA CUP, the only short-term League of Legends (LoL) cup tournament in Korea to be broadcast exclusively on Disney+, will begin on July 20.

Following the successful collaboration established last year, the LoL KeSPA CUP will continue its exclusive partnership with Disney+ this year. By streaming on Disney+ for the second consecutive year, the KeSPA CUP aims to provide a new viewing experience for esports fans while demonstrating the potential for expanding esports content across platforms. All matches of the 2026 LoL KeSPA CUP, held both online and offline, will be available to watch on Disney+.

The 2026 LoL KeSPA CUP will feature all 10 teams from the League of Legends Champions Korea (LCK). The tournament will run for approximately one month, from July 20 to August 18, with matches held every Monday and Tuesday. The preliminary rounds through the first round of the final Stage 2 will be held online from July 20 to August 11. The second round of Stage 2 and the final championship match will take place offline at the Daejeon Esports Arena in Yuseong, Daejeon, on August 17 and 18, respectively.

The tournament is divided into preliminary and final stages. In the preliminaries, the participating teams will be split into two groups of five and compete in a three-day single round-robin format, with the top four teams from each group advancing to the finals. The finals consist of Stage 1 and Stage 2. Stage 1 will follow a modified Swiss format, starting with matchups between teams that placed similarly in the preliminaries, and will span four rounds to determine which teams advance to Stage 2. The final stage, Stage 2, consists of three rounds, each played as a best-of-five series to determine the ultimate winner.

In addition to the prize pool, the Korea e-Sports Association plans to make a donation to Save the Children in the name of the participating teams and the association. The funds will be used to support medical expenses for children from vulnerable backgrounds. To allow esports fans to participate in this charitable cause, the association will also introduce individual donation programs and various events. This year's tournament is particularly meaningful as it takes the form of a charity event, extending the passion of esports into social value.

The 2026 LoL KeSPA CUP is hosted by the Korea e-Sports Association, co-organized by the Korea e-Sports Association and the Daejeon Information & Culture Industry Promotion Agency, exclusively broadcast by Disney+, and sponsored by Logitech G. The total prize pool is ₩52 million.
